estimate_basis.fx_assumption: Anthropic list price; no enterprise discount applied (FORA is pre-revenue)

stage_purpose.Architecture: Sub-goal 2.1 (FORA-27) — turn this Epic into an architecture ADR + design
stage_purpose.QA: Eval set + integration tests + prompt-injection regression (per SEC-2 / Q7)
stage_purpose.Security: Verify P0 controls (cross-tenant, audit completeness, secrets ownership, safety eval set)
stage_purpose.DevOps: Per-tenant budget gate, degraded-mode fallback, audit sink
stage_purpose.Docs: Knowledge Layer updates + per-story README + usage walkthrough

estimate_basis.estimation_method: Story-effort-in-weeks × stage-multiplier × dollar-per-engineer-week + token-cost-for-AI-assisted-work. Engineer-week cost = $8,000 fully-loaded (illustrative; FORA internal). Token cost is added on top. Ranges reflect p50/p95.
caveats: Cost is a forward estimate, not a contracted budget. The Architect will refine during 2.1.
Per-run cost (S5 generator) is bounded by the Draft PRD §8 hard ceiling of $5.00; that is captured separately under per_run_costs.
Q6 (secrets client) and Q7 (safety eval set) are tracked as additional cost lines — if unresolved they push Security cost upward.

Architecture.scope: 8 story ADRs + cross-cutting decisions (runtime vs prompt enforcement, schema versioning, audit sink topology, interaction schema, per-tenant namespace, degraded-mode fallback, secrets-ownership path)
Architecture.notes: Token cost is dwarfed by human cost; one architect-week + 2-4 dev-weeks of architecture review.

Dev.scope: 8 stories; per-story: design doc, implementation, unit tests, code review
Dev.notes: S5 is the largest story (L = 4 weeks). Critical-path story S5 cannot parallelize; S2/S3 can run in parallel after S1.

QA.scope: Eval set (per Q7: 5 prompt-injection + 5 role-violation cases), integration tests for the lint step, prompt-output regression for S5, interaction acceptance for S6
QA.notes: Q7 (safety eval set seed) is included here.

Security.scope: Verify P0 controls (R2.1 allow-list, R3.2 cross-project Jira, R5.1 PRD hallucination, R7.1 audit completeness, R7.2 cross-tenant audit, R7.3 secret-in-prompt, R8.1 cross-tenant, R8.2 budget bypass). Plus Q6 secrets-ownership path resolution and ADR.
Security.notes: P0 controls dominate this stage's cost. If Q6 unresolved by Architecture handoff, add 1-2 engineer-weeks ($8-16K) to file the secrets-client ticket.

DevOps.scope: Per-tenant budget gate (S8), degraded-mode fallback (S3), audit sink topology (S7), runbook for 'agent-loop run hangs' (per FORA-60 §9.2 gap)
DevOps.notes: Cost sensitive to whether S8's per-tenant budget gate is a new piece of infra or a configuration on the existing budget gate.

Docs.scope: Knowledge Layer updates for S1-S8; per-story README; usage walkthrough (Slack-form → console → API); PM-facing runbook
Docs.notes: Doc-agent already in flight per FORA-23.

epic_total_cost_usd.note: p95 ≈ $320K. Architect will refine during 2.1; this is the planning envelope.

token_cost_only_usd.note: Token cost is < 0.05 % of the epic total — human engineering cost dominates. AI assistance saves maybe 20-30 % vs all-human, but the stage gates are human-owned.

engineer_weeks_total.note: Roughly 6-10 engineer-months end-to-end. With 3 parallel engineers, calendar time is ~3-5 months.

per_run_costs.context: Per-run cost for the actual Forge Ideation run that produces a Draft PRD (the S5 generator, dominated by token spend).
per_run_costs.alignment_with_draft_prd_section_8: Per-run p50 $0.47 ≈ Draft PRD target $0.50 (within 6 %). Per-run p95 $2.85 < target $3.00. Hard ceiling $5.00 enforced.

Q6_secrets_client.description: If unresolved by Architecture handoff, file a secrets-client ticket and add it to Dev + Security stages.

Q7_safety_eval_set.description: Seed 5 prompt-injection + 5 role-violation cases before first design-partner run. Already included in QA stage above; if it slips past the design-partner run date, add to QA scope.

Q8_agent_runtime_adr.description: Add an ADR candidate resolving the TS-vs-Python divergence. Cost is < 1 engineer-week; absorbed in Architecture stage.

audit.method: Story-effort-in-weeks × stage-multiplier × dollar-per-engineer-week + token-cost-for-AI-assisted-work. Engineer-week = $8,000 fully loaded (illustrative). Token costs from Anthropic list price (fable-5 primary). p50/p95 ranges reflect planning uncertainty.
audit.confidence: medium — Architect will refine in Sub-goal 2.1. The estimate is wide because the per-story implementation approach has not yet been designed.
